name: frontier-alpha-ux-optimization-round-2
description: Round 2 iterative UX optimization — dark mode chart colors, large page decomposition, scroll-to-top, form validation polish, empty state CTAs, mobile nav. Surgical improvements, no rebuilds.
constraints: NO full rewrites — iterative changes only
ALL existing tests must pass after every story
ALL colors must use CSS variables
Zero visual regression on light mode
Build must pass: npm run build

userStories.title: Chart color palette CSS variables for dark mode
userStories.description: All chart components use hardcoded hex colors that don't adapt to dark mode. Create a chart color palette in CSS variables and update all chart components to read them via getComputedStyle.
acceptanceCriteria: New CSS variables in :root and .dark: --chart-primary, --chart-secondary, --chart-accent, --chart-danger, --chart-purple, --chart-cyan (6 chart colors)
Dark mode variants are lighter/brighter for readability on dark backgrounds
EquityCurve uses CSS variables via getComputedStyle (no hardcoded #60a5fa, #3b82f6, #0F1219, #ffffff)
MonteCarloChart uses CSS variables (no hardcoded #f87171, #dc2626, #60a5fa, #3b82f6)
WeightAllocation SEGMENT_COLORS reads from CSS variables
EpisodePerformanceChart uses CSS variables (no hardcoded #818cf8, #4f46e5, #8b5cf6)
FactorWeightHeatmap uses color-mix with CSS variables instead of hardcoded RGB
All charts look correct in both light and dark mode
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Remaining hardcoded colors in Social, Alerts, ML pages
userStories.description: Fix scattered hardcoded colors: Social.tsx mixed gradient (#a855f7), Alerts.tsx rgb() emoji colors, ML.tsx gradient mix. All should use CSS variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Social.tsx gradient uses only CSS variables (no hardcoded #a855f7)
Alerts.tsx priority colors use CSS variables or color-mix (no hardcoded rgb())
ML.tsx gradient uses only CSS variables
Zero hardcoded hex or rgb() colors in these 3 files
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Tree-shake d3 imports and add scroll-to-top on route change
userStories.description: Options.tsx imports all of d3 but only uses scaleSequential and interpolateViridis. Replace with specific imports. Add scroll-to-top behavior on route change in Layout.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: Options.tsx uses import { scaleSequential } from 'd3-scale' and import { interpolateViridis } from 'd3-scale-chromatic' instead of import * as d3
Layout.tsx adds useEffect that scrolls to top on location.pathname change
Scroll-to-top uses window.scrollTo(0, 0) — instant, not smooth (avoid jank)
No regression in Options page d3 visualizations
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Decompose Options.tsx into sub-components
userStories.description: Options.tsx is 1453 lines. Extract 4 major sections: OptionsChain, GreeksHeatmap, VolSurface, StrategySelector. Keep Options.tsx as orchestrator with tab switching logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Options.tsx reduced to <500 lines — orchestrator with tab state and data fetching
OptionsChain.tsx: chain table with filtering, sorting, strike selection
GreeksHeatmap.tsx: Greeks visualization with d3
VolSurface.tsx: 3D implied volatility surface projection
StrategySelector.tsx: strategy builder with leg editor and payoff preview
Each sub-component wrapped with React.memo()
Zero visual change — Options page looks identical
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Decompose ML.tsx into sub-components
userStories.description: ML.tsx is 728 lines. Extract RegimeDetector, ModelVersions, FactorAttribution. Keep ML.tsx as orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: ML.tsx reduced to <350 lines — orchestrator with section state
RegimeDetector.tsx: regime visualization with transition probability matrix
ModelVersions.tsx: model comparison table with version history
FactorAttribution.tsx: waterfall chart with factor contribution breakdown
Each sub-component wrapped with React.memo()
Zero visual change
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Form validation polish — Login and Signup
userStories.description: Add autofocus, inline validation, password strength meter, and loading states to auth forms.
acceptanceCriteria: LoginForm: email input has autoFocus
LoginForm: email validates format on blur (shows red border + 'Invalid email' if bad format)
LoginForm: submit button shows spinner and disables during login
SignupForm: real-time password match indicator (green check when passwords match)
SignupForm: password strength meter below password field (weak/medium/strong with color bar)
SignupForm: submit disabled until all fields valid
Both forms: inputs have autocomplete attributes (email, current-password, new-password)
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Improve empty state CTAs and error differentiation
userStories.description: Add actionable CTAs to empty states that currently have none. Differentiate error messages by type (network, auth, server).
acceptanceCriteria: EmptyAlerts has 'Create Alert' CTA button with onClick navigation to alerts config
EmptyEarnings has 'Add Holdings' CTA button
DataLoadError differentiates error types: network → connection message, 401 → re-auth message, 5xx → server message
DataLoadError shows appropriate icon per error type (wifi-off, lock, server)
All CTAs use Button component with proper styling
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Mobile navigation — bottom sheet for secondary pages
userStories.description: MobileNav only shows 5 pages. Add a 'More' button that opens a BottomSheet with the remaining 9+ pages.
acceptanceCriteria: 6th nav item is 'More' with MoreHorizontal icon from lucide-react
Tapping 'More' opens BottomSheet with remaining pages: Factors, Earnings, Backtest, ML, Options, Tax, Social, Settings, Help
Each item in BottomSheet has icon + label, tapping navigates and closes sheet
Active page highlighted in BottomSheet list
BottomSheet respects safe-area-inset-bottom
48px minimum tap targets on all items
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes

userStories.title: Add page title in mobile header + useEffect cleanup audit
userStories.description: Show current page title in mobile header for context. Audit and fix useEffect cleanup gaps across components.
acceptanceCriteria: Header shows current page title on mobile (e.g., 'Dashboard', 'Portfolio') derived from location.pathname
Page title hidden on desktop (already has sidebar for context)
Help.tsx setTimeout has proper cleanup (clearTimeout in useEffect return)
No orphaned timers or event listeners in any modified file
npx vitest run passes, npm run build passes
